beats.text: You've been working with arrays -- describing them by rows or columns, writing expressions for what you see, finding products. Let's see how it's going.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect values on first attempt

steps.text: Let's look closely. Count the rows first, then how many dots are in each row.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect values on second attempt

params.description: Both rows of the array highlight to emphasize the row structure.

steps.text: Here's a hint: there are 2 rows. Each row has 7 dots. The first number tells us how many rows.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect values three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System counts the rows: 1, 2. Then counts dots in one row: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. First, I count the rows: 1, 2. That's 2 rows. Then I count the dots in one row: 7 dots. So the expression is 2 times 7.

incorrects.description: Student selected 5 × 4. This describes the array by rows, not columns.

params.description: System highlights columns, one at a time.

steps.text: That's the rows way. Look at columns now. How many columns are there? Each column has how many dots?

incorrects.description: Student selected 4 × 4. This matches the number of columns but not the number in each column.

steps.text: You're right that there are 4 columns. But count how many dots are in each column. Is it 4 or 5?

incorrects.description: Student selected 5 × 5. This matches the number in each column but not the number of columns.

steps.text: You're right that each column has 5 dots. But count the columns. Are there 5 columns or 4 columns?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System highlights columns one at a time, then shows 4 × 5.

steps.text: Let me show you how to think about this. I count the columns: 1, 2, 3, 4. Four columns. Each column has 5 dots. So by columns, it's 4 times 5. The answer is 4 × 5.

incorrects.description: Student placed an incorrect equation on first attempt

steps.text: Let's look closely. Count the rows, then count how many dots are in each row.

incorrects.description: Student placed an incorrect equation on second attempt

params.description: Array highlights row structure. 6 horizontal rows visible.

steps.text: Here's a hint: there are 6 rows. Each row has 3 dots. That gives you the first two numbers in the equation.

incorrects.description: Student placed an incorrect equation three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System counts rows: highlights each of the 6 rows in sequence, showing 3 dots per row.
System places tiles: 6 in groups slot, 3 in items slot, 18 in total slot.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. I count the rows. There are 6 rows. I count the dots in one row. There are 3 dots. 6 times 3 equals 18. The equation is 6 times 3 equals 18.
